name: autonomous-loop
description: Claudecode /goal autonomous self-prompting loop. /goal <condition> registers a Stop hook that re-prompts with the condition after each turn ends; the agent iterates until goal_status.met == true or /goal clear fires. The recording uses a goal that converges in 2-3 iterations.

metadata.notes:
agent_supports: no
daemon_capability: n/a
driver_capability: ready

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: no
daemon_capability: n/a
driver_capability: ready

## Reasoning

The `autonomous-loop` scenario exercises the `autonomous_mode` primitive: the agent is given a goal/condition and self-prompts through internally-generated turns until the goal is met, with no user prompt separating iterations (Codex `/goal`, Plandex full-auto, SWE-agent, Goose recipe). Aider 0.86.2 has no such feature.

**No goal-driven / self-prompting surface in aider 0.86.2:**

- No `/goal`, `--auto`, `--autonomous`, or `--full-auto`. `aider --help` exposes only `--auto-commits`, `--auto-lint`, `--auto-test`, `--auto-accept-architect`, and `--yes-always` — all of which suppress *confirmation prompts*; none drive a self-prompting loop toward a goal.
- The in-chat slash commands (`/code`, `/ask`, `/architect`, `/run`, `/test`, `/lint`, …) are all single-shot; none registers a re-prompting hook. There is no `/goal` equivalent.
- A keyword scan of the installed aider package (`grep -riE 'autonomous|self.?prompt|full.?auto|goal|swe.?agent'`) surfaced only substring false positives (`prompt_pending`, `prompt_session` in `io.py`/`gui.py`). No autonomous-loop machinery exists.

**What aider *does* have is a bounded self-correction loop, which is NOT the scenario.** `Coder.run_one` (`coders/base_coder.py`) wraps `send_message` in a `while message:` loop driven by `self.reflected_message`, hard-capped at `max_reflections = 3` ("Only 3 reflections allowed, stopping."). The only things that set `reflected_message` are *technical failures*: a malformed edit block, an auto-lint error (`--auto-lint`), an auto-test failure (`--auto-test`), a needs-to-add-files message, or an edit-application exception. This is post-edit error recovery, not iteration toward a user-stated goal until `goal_status.met == true`. It is bounded (≤3), failure-triggered, and terminates on success — the opposite of the open-ended self-prompting the scenario requires.

**Architect mode is a fixed two-step chain, not a loop.** `ArchitectCoder.reply_completed` runs the architect model once to plan, then — gated on `confirm_ask("Edit the files?")` unless `--auto-accept-architect` — spawns one editor coder to apply, then returns to the prompt. That is the "limited multi-step" the prior matrix note referred to; it is two model calls within a single user turn, with no goal condition and no self-re-prompting. It does not satisfy `autonomous_mode`.

The upstream HISTORY/changelog confirms the design intent: aider is an "AI pair programming" tool that "consistently requires user confirmation between iterations"; the only autonomy is bounded post-edit self-correction (auto-lint/test/reflection), explicitly scoped to immediate technical failures rather than multi-step goal pursuit.

## Observability (daemon_capability: n/a)

Because aider lacks the feature, `daemon_capability` is `n/a` — there is no behavior to observe (`agent_supports: no`). `driver_capability` is `ready`: the autonomous-loop recipe never runs, so the driver is not exercised, and the interactive driver (`scripts/drive-aider-interactive.sh`) implements every step a hypothetical run would use (send / slash / wait_turn) without depending on a gapped primitive — there is no driver step blocking this cell. Per the #476 frozen-cell convention (`agent_supports: no` ⇒ `daemon_capability: n/a`, `driver_capability: ready`), the cell is frozen at Stage 1 for lack of the agent feature, not for any daemon or tooling limit.

For completeness on the transport: aider's Source is `FilesUnderCWD` over the markdown `.aider.chat.history.md`, and the adapter parser (`core/adapters/inbound/agents/aider/parser.go`) opens a turn on a `#### ` user-prompt line and closes it via `IdleFlush` after 1500ms of transcript quiet. Even the reflection loop is invisible to this transport: reflections emit no `#### ` line, so the daemon would see a single `working` span that ends on idle — it could never distinguish a 3-reflection self-correction from one long model call. But this is moot: the agent does not support the scenario, so no recording is produced.

## Capability key

The scenario's **Primitive exercised** maps to `autonomous_mode`. `replaydata/agents/aider/capabilities.json` has no `autonomous_mode` key (and no agent's capabilities.json declares one as a literal flag — it is the conceptual primitive). Aider's nearest flags are `headless_mode: true` (the scenario `requires`) and the `--auto-*` confirmation-suppression switches, none of which is autonomous self-prompting.
caveats: Aider's bounded reflection loop (max_reflections=3, failure-triggered) is the closest thing to autonomy but is error-recovery, not goal-driven self-prompting; do not mistake it for autonomous_mode in future re-assessments.
Architect mode's plan→edit is a fixed two-step chain within one user turn, not a loop — this is the 'limited multi-step' the prior matrix note flagged; it does not satisfy the scenario.

sources.note: run_one() reflection loop: `while message:` re-prompts on self.reflected_message, hard-capped at max_reflections=3 ('Only 3 reflections allowed, stopping.'). reflected_message is set only by lint/test/edit-failure paths (lines ~1606/1622/2315/2327) — bounded self-correction, not goal-driven autonomy.

ArchitectCoder.reply_completed: single architect plan then one editor coder to apply, gated on confirm_ask('Edit the files?') unless --auto-accept-architect. Fixed two-step, no goal condition, no self-re-prompt.

sources.ref: aider --help (aider 0.86.2 binary at /Users/ingo/.local/bin/aider) + commands.py slash-command scan
sources.note: Only --auto-commits/--auto-lint/--auto-test/--auto-accept-architect/--yes-always (confirmation suppressors); slash commands /code /ask /architect /run /test /lint etc. are single-shot. No /goal, --auto, --autonomous, or --full-auto. grep -riE 'autonomous|self.?prompt|full.?auto|goal|swe.?agent' over the package found only prompt_pending/prompt_session false positives. Step 2.5 strings/docs scan supporting agent_supports: no.

Changelog/design: aider 'consistently requires user confirmation between iterations'; only autonomy is bounded post-edit self-correction (auto-lint/test/reflection) scoped to immediate technical failures, not multi-step goal pursuit. No autonomous/agentic-loop feature shipped.

Transport read for completeness: FilesUnderCWD markdown; turn opens on '#### ' user line, closes via IdleFlush after 1500ms quiet. Reflections write no '#### ' line so would be invisible anyway — moot since agent_supports is no, daemon_capability is n/a.

metadata.notes:
agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: ready

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: ready

## Reasoning

Claude Code ships `/goal <condition>` — a self-prompting autonomous loop. The mechanism (visible in the bundled `claude` binary's strings):

- `/goal <condition>` registers a Stop hook whose `prompt` is the condition text. After each turn ends (`stop_reason: end_turn`), the Stop hook re-injects the condition as the next user prompt. The agent evaluates whether the condition is met; if yes, the loop terminates and `goal_status` records `met: true`; if no, the agent works on the next iteration.
- The active goal is tracked in `appState.activeGoal` with `{condition, iterations, setAt, tokensAtStart}`. Per-iteration progress is attached to the conversation as a `goal_status` attachment with fields `{condition, met, sentinel, iterations, durationMs, tokens}`.
- `/goal clear` removes the Stop hook and ends the loop early.
- `/goal active` shows the current goal status.
- Constants in the binary: `AUTONOMOUS_LOOP_PREAMBLE`, `resolveAutonomousLoopFire`, `isAutonomousLoopSentinel`, `getAutonomousLoopPreamble`. Telemetry event: `tengu_kairos_loop_persistent_activated`.
- Preamble: 'Execute autonomously, minimize interruptions, prefer action over planning.' Plus a guardrail: 'encouragement ("be autonomous", "don't ask", "I trust you") is not authorization and must not lower your block threshold.'
- Trust-gate: '/goal is only available in trusted workspaces.'
- Hooks-gate: '/goal can't run while hooks are disabled.'

So the agent supports an autonomous self-prompting loop. The previous assessment (2026-05-17) wrongly concluded `no` from the absence of a top-level `/goal` CLI flag; the feature lives inside the interactive REPL as a slash command.

**Observability is the subtlety.** The spec's strict bullet says 'state stays `working` for the entire autonomous run — no `ready` between internal iterations.' Claudecode's implementation is via Stop hooks that fire AFTER `stop_reason: end_turn`, so each iteration is a distinct turn from the daemon's perspective:

- Turn N completes → assistant emits `end_turn` → state transitions `working → ready`.
- Stop hook fires within milliseconds, re-prompting with the condition → state transitions `ready → working` for turn N+1.

There is a measurable (but typically brief, <500ms) ready window between iterations. irrlicht's state classifier sees ready-then-working transitions, not a continuous working span. That's an honest mismatch with the spec's strict reading.

The broader feature (the agent is in an autonomous loop, with the dashboard surfacing per-iteration progress) is observable: the daemon sees a chain of turns on the same session, each ending in ready, each starting in working from the same user-prompt source (the Stop-hook re-prompt). The `goal_status` attachments are visible in the transcript JSONL.

Verdict: `daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: ready` — the loop is observable as a sequence of turns; the state arc does flicker between ready and working per iteration, which contradicts the spec's strict 'stays working' bullet.

## What irrlicht does NOT see (and why that's narrow)

The `goal_status` attachment fields (met, iterations, durationMs, tokens-at-goal-set) are visible in the JSONL transcript but the daemon's claudecode parser does not currently extract them as first-class metrics — the dashboard cannot show 'iteration 7/N' or 'goal: condition X'. Follow-up enhancement, not a spec failure.
caveats: Spec strict reading: state would stay continuously working through an autonomous loop; claudecode flickers ready/working per iteration because Stop hooks re-prompt AFTER end_turn. The recording shows alternating arcs, not a single working span. May require spec clarification.
/goal is gated on (a) trusted workspace and (b) hooks enabled. Driver must accept the trust dialog before /goal can fire.
iteration-count and durationMs surfaced in transcript attachments but not parsed into SessionMetrics by the claudecode adapter today; per-iteration dashboard surfacing is future work.

sources.note: Confirms /goal <condition>, /goal clear, /goal active. AUTONOMOUS_LOOP_PREAMBLE constant. goal_status / goal_set / goal_met telemetry events. Stop-hook re-prompt mechanism (function QiH registers prompt-type Stop hook with goal condition).
Feature: Autonomous loop (self-prompting) L160-168 — strict 'stays working' bullet is the source of the partial verdict.
State classifier treats each end_turn as a working→ready transition; no special-case for Stop-hook re-prompt coalescence.

preconditions: claude CLI on PATH and authenticated
irrlichd is recording (--attach or isolated mode)
/goal requires a trusted workspace and hooks enabled (default settings satisfy both)
setup: Driver generates a fresh UUID + cwd; trust dialog is accepted automatically

script.text: /goal You have run two bash commands so far this session: `echo iter1` and `echo iter2`, in that exact order. Run them one at a time across iterations.

verify: events.jsonl contains at least 2 working→ready transitions on the same UUID — the /goal set + at least one Stop-hook iteration
final state of the session is ready after /goal clear
no state_transition to waiting (no permission gates expected)

metadata.notes:
agent_supports: partial
daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: ready

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: partial
daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: ready

## Reasoning

Codex (0.130.0) ships **no built-in autonomous self-prompting loop** — there is no `/goal`-style construct like Claude Code's Stop-hook re-prompt, no Plandex-style full-auto, and `autonomous_mode` is not among codex's declared capabilities (capabilities.json has no autonomous_mode key; the closest, headless_mode, is single-shot `codex exec`). Codex cannot drive ITSELF through an entire task goal with no user prompts between iterations.

What codex CAN model — and what the scenario-meanings 'Essence' actually targets for an adapter that lacks native autonomy — is an **externally-driven loop**: a driver/harness feeds successive prompts to the same live REPL session in a loop, each prompt advancing one iteration of a single ongoing goal. This is the honest codex analogue of the autonomous-loop primitive: the irrlicht-relevant behavior is whether the session cycles cleanly across internally-similar, back-to-back iterations without getting stuck in working and without false flips, NOT whether the model self-prompts.

So agent_supports is **partial**: codex supports the loop only when the loop body is supplied externally (driver send → wait_turn → short sleep → send …), not autonomously. This mirrors how the claudecode sibling already had to relax the spec's strict 'stays continuously working' bullet: even claudecode's /goal flickers ready↔working per Stop-hook iteration. Codex's externally-driven loop produces the same observable arc — a chain of clean per-iteration working→ready cycles on one rollout session — so it is captured the same way the claudecode sibling captures it.

## Observability

irrlicht_observes: **yes**. Each loop iteration is a normal codex turn on the live rollout session: ready → working at iteration start, working → ready at iteration end. The daemon sees the proc-PID pre-session born ready, the rollout session reconcile (new session_id) born ready, pid bound, then N clean per-iteration working/ready arcs on the SAME rollout session_id, then process_exited when the driver tears the REPL down. No `waiting` state (codex has no permission gates: permission_hooks=false). This is structurally identical to the already-recorded codex multi-turn-conversation cell, which validates the same per-turn arc shape — the only semantic difference is intent (loop iterations of one goal vs three unrelated prompts), which the spec captures as a no-false-flip / no-stuck-working invariant. The thing irrlicht does NOT surface — a first-class 'iteration 3/N' counter — is not a codex construct at all (no goal_status attachment) and is out of scope for this primitive.

## Why not n/a

Not n/a: the externally-driven multi-turn loop is fully expressible with codex's interactive driver (send/slash/wait_turn/sleep all supported) and the resulting recording exercises exactly the irrlicht primitive the scenario cares about (clean cross-iteration cycling, no stuck-working, no false waiting). It is NOT an orchestrator-only concept (gastown) — a single codex session iterating under external prompting is a legitimate single-agent recording.
caveats: Codex has no native autonomous mode; the loop body must be supplied externally by the driver (send → wait_turn → sleep, repeated). The recording models an externally-driven loop, not codex self-prompting. Documented in the recipe description so a re-record diff doesn't read as a regression.

Spec strict reading ('state stays working for the entire run') does not hold for codex any more than it does for claudecode: the session cycles ready↔working per externally-fed iteration. The recording asserts clean per-iteration cycling plus the no-waiting / no-stuck invariants, not a single continuous working span.
Mind the classifier debounce: ≥4s sleep between wait_turn and the next send so consecutive iterations register as distinct working→ready arcs rather than one coalesced turn.

sources.note: No autonomous_mode key; headless_mode is single-shot codex exec, permission_hooks=false (no waiting gates), slash_commands=true, session_resume=true.

Essence + conceptual flow: the key irrlicht primitive is keeping/cycling session state across internally-generated iteration boundaries with no false flips. Strict 'stays working' bullet relaxed for adapters whose iterations are turn-delimited.

Sibling models the loop as per-iteration working→ready arcs (not a continuous working span) because Stop hooks re-prompt after end_turn — same observable shape the externally-driven codex loop produces.
Driver supports send|slash, wait_turn, sleep, interrupt, keys, reset_session, fork, exit_clean, resume — every step type the externally-driven loop recipe needs.

preconditions: codex CLI on PATH and authenticated
irrlichd is recording (--attach or isolated mode)
Codex has NO built-in autonomous self-prompting loop (no /goal, no full-auto); the loop is driven EXTERNALLY by the driver feeding successive prompts to the same live REPL session
setup: Driver launches one interactive codex REPL; the rollout session reconciles to its own session_id once the first prompt lands. No trust dialog / settings file (codex has none).

script.text: We are running an iterative loop toward one goal: print the numbers 1, 2, 3 one per iteration, never repeating a number. This is iteration 1 — run `echo 1` and report the next number you will print. Do not print the others yet.
Continue the loop: iteration 2. Run `echo 2` and report the next number. Do not print the final one yet.
Final loop iteration 3: run `echo 3`. The goal (1, 2, 3) is now complete — confirm and stop.

verify: events.jsonl contains the proc-PID pre-session born ready, then the rollout session reconcile (new session_id) born ready, then a pid_discovered for the rollout session
events.jsonl contains at least 2 working→ready transitions on the SAME rollout session_id — one clean cycle per externally-fed loop iteration
each iteration cycles ready→working→ready without the session sticking in working between iterations
final state of the session is ready after the last iteration, then process_exited on REPL teardown
no state_transition to waiting (codex has no permission gates)

metadata.notes:
agent_supports: unknown
daemon_capability: n/a
driver_capability: ready

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: unknown
daemon_capability: n/a
driver_capability: ready

## Reasoning

The `autonomous-loop` scenario requires the agent to self-prompt across internally-generated turn boundaries with **no user message between iterations** — the session must stay `working` for the whole autonomous run (per scenario-meanings: the canonical primitive is `autonomous_mode`, e.g. claudecode `/goal`, codex full-auto). Core OpenCode does NOT ship such a mode.

- A `strings $(which opencode)` scan for `autonomous`, `goal`, `self-prompt`, `full-auto`, `goal_status`, `continue until`, and slash-command markers came up empty — the only `goal`/`loop` hits are bundler / WASI / HTTP-508 ("Loop Detected") / Node event-loop noise from the bun-compiled binary, not a feature surface.
- OpenCode's documented model is turn-based: a turn ends at `session.idle` (step-finish) and the REPL waits for the next user prompt. There is no `/goal`, no `/loop`, and no `--auto` flag.
- The only autonomous self-prompting behaviors that exist ("Ralph Loop", "Ultrawork"/"Beast Mode") are THIRD-PARTY plugins (oh-my-opencode) that hook `session.idle` and re-call `session.prompt` with a CONTINUATION_PROMPT — they are not part of stock opencode. An upstream feature request to add a built-in `/loop` command (anomalyco/opencode#23578) is still OPEN, confirming core opencode lacks the primitive.

Because stock opencode has no documented autonomous self-prompting / goal-loop mode, `agent_supports` is `unknown` (a plugin-only path is not a stock-agent capability). With no agent-native loop to observe, the observability question is moot, so `daemon_capability` is `n/a`.

## Driver / observability note

If a future opencode version (or a sanctioned plugin) shipped a real headless-drivable loop, the daemon's observability would still need re-checking: opencode's adapter is `ProcessOwnedStore` (SQLite, `core/adapters/inbound/agents/opencode/agent.go`), where the daemon polls a structured store rather than tailing JSONL, so whether mid-loop iterations stay `working` is empirically uncertain and must be measured from a live recording — not predicted from docs. That re-assessment is out of scope while the agent itself lacks the mode.
caveats: Autonomous self-prompting in opencode exists only via third-party plugins (oh-my-opencode Ralph Loop / Ultrawork / Beast Mode), not stock opencode.
An upstream feature request for a built-in /loop command (anomalyco/opencode#23578) is still open — re-assess if it merges.

sources.note: features.headless_mode=false; no autonomous_mode key — stock opencode declares no autonomous-loop capability
Source is ProcessOwnedStore (SQLite); observability of mid-loop working state would need a live recording, not doc inference
official CLI docs — turn-based REPL, no /goal, no /loop, no --auto autonomous mode
open feature request to ADD a /loop command — confirms core opencode has no built-in autonomous loop
autonomous self-prompting is a third-party oh-my-opencode plugin hooking session.idle to re-prompt — not stock opencode

recipe.notes: scope_note: stock opencode has no documented autonomous self-prompting / goal-loop mode; a turn ends at session.idle (step-finish) and waits for the next user message — autonomous self-prompting exists only via third-party plugins (oh-my-opencode Ralph Loop / Ultrawork), and the upstream built-in /loop request (anomalyco/opencode#23578) is still open. Nothing autonomously recordable here. agent_supports=unknown (see assessment.json).

metadata.notes:
agent_supports: no
daemon_capability: n/a; driver_capability: n/a

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: no
daemon_capability: n/a; driver_capability: n/a

## Scenario

`autonomous-loop` exercises the **autonomous-mode / self-prompting** primitive (`autonomous_mode` in capabilities.json): the agent registers a goal/condition and then drives itself through internally-generated turn boundaries with no user prompt between iterations, while irrlicht keeps the session `working` for the entire run (no `ready` between internal iterations) until the goal is reached. The canonical example is Codex/claudecode `/goal <condition>` (a Stop hook that re-prompts after each turn), Plandex full-auto, SWE-agent, or a Goose recipe.

## agent_supports: no

pi 0.70.0 ships **no self-prompting / autonomous-goal mode**. This is confirmed at three layers, with the binary scan being authoritative:

1. **Binary keyword scan (Step 2.5 guard).** pi is a Node bundle; the `pi` wrapper symlinks to `@mariozechner/pi-coding-agent/dist/cli.js`. Scanning the whole `dist/` tree for the autonomous-loop vocabulary — `autonomous`, `self-prompt`, `/goal`, `goal_status`, `goal_set`, `goal_met`, `--auto`, `auto-loop`, `full-auto`, `max-iteration`, `iteration-limit`, `continue-until`, `loop-until`, `unattended`, `run-until` — returned **zero hits**. The only `goal`/`Goal` substrings live in `dist/core/compaction/` (summarization prompt text that references "the user's goal"), unrelated to a self-prompting loop. There is no `goal_status`/`goal_met` telemetry and no Stop-hook re-prompt machinery — the single `re-prompt` token appears only in a source map, not in the readable `.d.ts` API surface or live code. This is the same scan that, for claudecode, surfaces `/goal <condition>`, `AUTONOMOUS_LOOP_PREAMBLE`, and `goal_status` telemetry (the documented cautionary miss); for pi it comes up empty.

2. **Full slash-command catalog.** The complete command list extracted from the bundle is `/arminsayshi /changelog /clone /colors /compact /copy /debug /dementedelves /export /fork /hotkeys /import /login /logout /model /name /new /quit /reload /resume /scoped-models /session /settings /share /tree`. There is no `/goal`, `/auto`, or any autonomous-mode command.

3. **`pi --help`.** No `--goal` / `--auto` / autonomous flag exists; all flags are model/session/tools/thinking oriented. `--print, -p` is non-interactive single-shot (process prompt and exit), not a self-driving loop. The help text explicitly notes "Extensions can register additional flags (e.g., --plan from plan-mode extension)" — i.e. autonomy/plan are extension territory, not stock pi. The pi README frames pi as turn-driven and "ships defaults but skips features" you'd build via a third-party package.

`capabilities.json` for pi has **no `autonomous_mode` key** (the primitive's capability anchor), consistent with the feature being absent.

## daemon_capability: n/a; driver_capability: n/a

Because the agent has no autonomous-loop feature, there is nothing for the daemon to observe — hence `n/a` rather than `no`. Grounding the transport read regardless: the pi adapter is `FilesUnderRoot` + `ExactName{pi}` (agent.go), and the parser (parser.go) emits `turn_done` **only** on `message.role==assistant && stopReason=="stop"`, and `user_message` on `role==user`. There is no event kind that distinguishes an internally self-generated next iteration from a user-prompted next turn. Even hypothetically, irrlicht has no signal that would prove "no `ready` between internal iterations" because pi never produces internally-generated turns. The `applicable` result is `n/a`: the agent layer is frozen, so no recipe or recording follows.

## Confirms reassessment

This ratifies the column reassessment (`agent_supports: no`, confidence 0.82) and the current coverage matrix (`no` / `n/a`). The binary-scan evidence raises confidence to 0.9 by closing the Step 2.5 "is it a hidden slash command?" question with a primary, empty result.
caveats: A third-party pi extension could add a self-prompting loop (pi's --help advertises extension-registered flags like --plan), but that is not stock pi and would need its own assessment.

The autonomous-loop primitive's capability anchor `autonomous_mode` is absent from pi's capabilities.json; a maintainer adding that key for cross-agent matrix completeness would set it to false for pi.

sources.note: Step 2.5 binary scan of the pi 0.70.0 dist bundle (cli.js symlink target): grep -riE 'autonomous|self-prompt|/goal|goal_status|goal_met|--auto|full-auto|max-iteration|loop-until' returned ZERO hits; only 'Goal' substrings are in core/compaction/ summarization prompts, not a loop feature.

Full slash-command catalog extracted from bundle: /changelog /clone /compact /copy /export /fork /hotkeys /import /login /logout /model /name /new /quit /reload /resume /scoped-models /session /settings /share /tree — no /goal or /auto.

No --goal/--auto/autonomous flag; flags are provider/model/session/tools/thinking oriented. --print/-p is single-shot non-interactive. Note: 'Extensions can register additional flags (e.g., --plan from plan-mode extension)' — autonomy is extension-only.
turn_done only on assistant stopReason==stop; user_message on role==user. No event kind distinguishes a self-generated iteration from a user-prompted turn — moot since pi has no autonomous loop.
No autonomous_mode key present for pi (the primitive's capability anchor is absent).
